description: Implement the Child Sub-Graph to handle isolated execution per job, wire up stub nodes for the processing pipeline, and configure the LangGraph SqliteSaver checkpointer for Human-in-the-Loop interrupts.

userStories.title: Define Child Sub-Graph Structure with Stub Nodes
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want to define the child graph with stub nodes for Evaluate, Clarify, Strategy, Review, Apply, Cover Letter, and PDF compilation so that the core Map-Reduce execution pipeline exists.
acceptanceCriteria: Given the child graph is being constructed, When adding nodes, Then stubs for evaluate_fit, clarification, strategy_generator, human_review, apply_changes, cover_letter, and pdf_compiler are added.
Given the nodes are added, When defining edges, Then they are linked sequentially from START to END (e.g. evaluate_fit -> clarification -> ...).

tasks.description: Create src/nodes/child_nodes.py with stub functions for the 7 processing nodes that return empty state updates.
Create src/child_graph.py (or update src/graph.py) to build child_graph using StateGraph(ChildGraphState).
Add all child stub nodes and define a linear sequential edge flow.
Write pytest to verify child_graph compiles successfully.

userStories.notes: We only need stubs for now. The complex logic for Evaluate, Strategy, and HITL interrupts will be added in E-03 and E-04.

userStories.title: Configure SQLite Checkpointer for Child Graph
userStories.description: As a systems engineer, I want to attach a SqliteSaver checkpointer to the compiled child graph so that long-running threads can persist state and pause at Human-in-the-Loop interrupts.
acceptanceCriteria: Given the application runs, When initializing the checkpointer, Then a SQLite database connection is established at data/checkpoints.sqlite.
Given the checkpointer is available, When compiling the child graph, Then it is configured with checkpointer=sqlite_saver.
Given the child graph is compiled, When execution reaches clarification or human_review, Then it halts via the interrupt_before setting.

tasks.description: Update src/child_graph.py to initialize SqliteSaver using from_conn_string('data/checkpoints.sqlite'). Ensure the data/ directory is created if missing.
Compile child_graph with the checkpointer and interrupt_before=['clarification', 'human_review'].
Write a test using MemorySaver (for testing) to demonstrate the graph pauses when reaching an interrupt node.

userStories.notes: Make sure to import SqliteSaver from langgraph.checkpoint.sqlite. MemorySaver can be used in unit tests to avoid disk I/O.

userStories.title: Connect Parent Graph to Child Graph via Send API
userStories.description: As a graph architect, I want the parent graph to dispatch jobs directly to the compiled child graph so that end-to-end processing triggers the child logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Given the ParentGraph is compiled, When map_to_job_processor returns Send objects, Then the target node is the compiled child_graph.
Given the ParentGraph executes, When the conditional edge maps to the child subgraph, Then the child subgraph runs with isolated state.

tasks.description: Import the compiled child_graph into src/graph.py.
Replace dummy_process_job in the ParentGraph builder with builder.add_node('child_graph', child_graph).
Update map_to_job_processor to use Send('child_graph', child_state) instead of Send('process_job', child_state).
Write/update integration test to verify the Parent graph correctly dispatches to the Child graph stubs without errors.

userStories.notes: Verify that LangGraph's Send API properly executes a compiled subgraph as a node.
